notes: Week-101: Reputation Score MVP, dashboard data refresh, CTO tech debt, invoice PDF export, agent leaderboard. Sprints 138-154 all confirmed done on server (week-99 + auto-sprints). These are the next highest-value product tasks.

tasks.description: Build Reputation Score MVP — GET /v1/reputation/:agentId returns computed reputation score based on payment history
Update dashboard frontend to consume new analytics endpoints — peak hours, invoice aging, payment lag, growth metrics
tasks.context:
The swarm has built 17 new analytics endpoints (sprints 138-154) but the dashboard frontend still shows basic stats from the original launch. This task connects the dashboard to the new data.

NEW ENDPOINTS TO WIRE (confirmed built in sprints 143-154):
  - GET /v1/metrics/peak-hours → { hour, count }[] — 24hr bar chart
  - GET /v1/invoices/stats/aging → aging buckets (0-7d, 7-30d, 30d+)
  - GET /v1/invoices/stats/payment-lag → { medianDays, avgDays, p90Days }
  - GET /v1/metrics/growth → { week, count, amount }[] weekly growth
  - GET /v1/invoices/stats/void → void rate stats

TARGET FILE: frontend/app/dashboard/page.tsx (or wherever the main dashboard is)

BEFORE WRITING:
  1. Read frontend/app/dashboard/page.tsx to understand current structure
  2. Read frontend/lib/api-client.ts to see existing API call patterns

ADD to api-client.ts (after existing fetch functions):
  - fetchPeakHours(): Promise<{ hour: number; count: number }[]>
  - fetchInvoiceAging(): Promise<{ bucket: string; count: number; totalAmount: number }[]>
  - fetchPaymentLag(): Promise<{ medianDays: number; avgDays: number; p90Days: number }>
  - fetchGrowthMetrics(): Promise<{ week: string; count: number; amount: number }[]>

ADD to dashboard page — 2 new stat cards (keep it minimal, don't redesign):
  Card 1 — 'Payment Speed': show medianDays from payment-lag (e.g. 'Avg 2.4 days to settle')
  Card 2 — 'Peak Hour': show top hour from peak-hours (e.g. 'Peak: 14:00 UTC')

Do NOT add charts — just stat cards. Max 60 new lines across both files.
After: run npx tsc --noEmit in frontend/ dir.

tasks.description: Split large frontend files — api-keys page and any other files over 300 lines (CTO-20260215-006)
tasks.context:
CTO flagged: frontend files are growing beyond the 200-line one-file-per-call architecture limit. Priority target: api-keys page.

STEP 1 — Find large files:
  Run: find frontend/app -name '*.tsx' -o -name '*.ts' | xargs wc -l | sort -rn | head -10
  List files over 250 lines.

STEP 2 — For api-keys page (frontend/app/api-keys/page.tsx or similar):
  Read the file. Split into:
  - page.tsx (the page component — data fetching + layout, max 100 lines)
  - components/ApiKeyList.tsx (the table/list component)
  - components/CreateApiKeyModal.tsx (the creation form/modal if it exists)
  Keep all existing functionality — this is purely structural refactoring.

STEP 3 — Check if other dashboard pages exceed 300 lines and apply same split pattern.

RULE: Do NOT change any logic, just extract components. Each extracted component gets its own file under the page's directory (e.g., frontend/app/api-keys/components/).

AFTER: Run npx tsc --noEmit in frontend/ — 0 new errors. Verify UI still works by checking imports.

tasks.description: GET /v1/invoices/:id/pdf — generate and return a PDF invoice for download
tasks.context:
User-facing feature: agents and their customers need to download PDF invoices for accounting.

IMPLEMENTATION:
  Use the 'pdfkit' npm package (check if already installed: grep pdfkit backend/package.json).
  If not installed: add to dependencies — but DO NOT run npm install; add to package.json manually and note that the server will install on next deploy.

FILE: backend/src/routes/invoices.ts — add after existing routes:
  GET /:id/pdf
  1. Fetch invoice by id from Prisma (include customer, seller info)
  2. If not found: return 404
  3. Generate PDF with pdfkit:
     - Header: 'INVOICE' title + Invoica branding
     - Invoice details: invoiceNumber, date, dueDate, status
     - Seller info: agentId / sellerName
     - Customer info: customerName, customerEmail
     - Line items: serviceDescription, subtotal, tax (if any), total
     - Payment details: paymentAddress, chain, txHash (if settled)
     - Footer: 'Generated by Invoica — invoica.ai'
  4. Set headers: Content-Type: application/pdf, Content-Disposition: attachment; filename='INV-xxx.pdf'
  5. Pipe PDF stream to response

KEEP IT SIMPLE: flat single-page PDF, no complex layout. Max 80 lines for the route handler.

TEST FILE: backend/src/routes/__tests__/invoice-pdf.test.ts
  - 200-returns-pdf-content-type
  - 200-returns-content-disposition
  - 404-not-found
  - 500-db-error

AFTER: npx tsc --noEmit — 0 new errors.

tasks.description: GET /v1/agents/leaderboard — top agents ranked by total settled revenue with reputation scores
tasks.context:
Foundation endpoint for the Agent Marketplace (Priority 2 in roadmap). Surfaces top-performing agents ranked by revenue.

RESPONSE shape:
  {
    agents: [
      {
        rank: number;
        agentId: string;
        totalRevenue: number;      // sum of SETTLED+COMPLETED invoice amounts
        invoiceCount: number;
        settlementRate: number;    // 0–1
        reputationScore: number;   // 0–100 (same computation as REPUTATION-001)
        reputationTier: string;    // bronze/silver/gold/platinum
      }
    ];
    total: number;                  // total number of agents
    computedAt: string;
  }

QUERY PARAMS:
  ?limit=N (default 10, max 50)
  ?tier=gold|platinum (optional filter by reputation tier)

IMPLEMENTATION:
  FILE: backend/src/routes/agents.ts — add GET /leaderboard before /:agentId routes
  1. Query: GROUP BY agentId, SUM amount WHERE status IN [SETTLED, COMPLETED], ORDER BY totalRevenue DESC, LIMIT N
  2. For each agent, compute reputation score inline (reuse same formula as REPUTATION-001 — extract to a shared helper if possible)
  3. Return ranked array

NOTE: If REPUTATION-001 is not yet merged, implement the score computation inline (don't create a dependency blocker). The inline formula is described in REPUTATION-001 context above.

TESTS: backend/src/routes/__tests__/agent-leaderboard.test.ts
  - 200-returns-agents-array
  - 200-has-rank-field
  - 200-has-reputation-score
  - 200-respects-limit
  - 200-empty-when-no-agents
  - 500-db-error

AFTER: npx tsc --noEmit — 0 new errors.
